title: Jelleo hypothesis library entry
description: Schema for entries under the top-level 'hypotheses:' list in a hypotheses.yaml file. See docs/HYPOTHESIS_SCHEMA.md and https://jelleo.com/methodology.html#scoping for prose documentation.

id.description: Unique hypothesis identifier. Convention: H<n>-<slug>. Stable across cycles.

class.description: Hypothesis class — drives Layer-2 dispatch path selection.

claim.description: The falsifiable claim in plain English. Phrased so a clean negative result strengthens the disclosure.

severity.description: Optional declared severity floor. Auto-derived severity may promote (never demote) this value.

applies_to.description: List of protocol names this hypothesis applies to. Use ['*'] to apply universally. Default if absent: ['*'].

scope_conditions.description: List of predicate names. Hypothesis loads only if every predicate evaluates true under target's workspace.json conditions. Vocabulary: has_insurance_pool, has_haircut_accounting, perpetual_funding, uses_pyth_oracle, uses_switchboard_oracle, liquidation_engine, multi_market, clob_orderbook, amm_constant_product, flash_loan, multi_collateral, cross_program_invocation_heavy.

bug_class.description: Generalized bug-class identifier for cross-protocol propagation. When this hypothesis confirms TRUE, the propagation engine dispatches the same bug_class against every protocol in applies_to.

target_file.description: Anchor file path within the target's engine repo. Used by the recon agent to focus its initial reading window.

relevant_constants.description: Free-form text listing bound constants the agent should consider.

relevant_instructions.description: Free-form list of public instructions / helpers the hypothesis touches.
